description: Fast-paced, 6-a-side lacrosse format played on smaller field with shot clock, no faceoffs, and free substitution

rules: Players can enter and exit freely through substitution line
Higher substitution frequency expected due to pace
substitutions.notes: Teams typically rotate every 4-5 possessions or ~3 minutes to manage fatigue

faceoffs.team_rotation: Team that scored gains possession for next goal; losing team starts next possession after goal
faceoffs.game_start: Alternating possession arrows used; home team typically starts with ball

possession_arrows.implementation: Arrow on scorer's table points to team with next possession after goal
possession_arrows.start_of_game: Home team typically starts with possession

crease.description: Area around goal where only goalie allowed

out_of_bounds.exception: Attacking team caused it - defending team possession

ball_possession.ground_balls: Fastest player controls; critical in small-field game

goal.description: Ball completely enters goal below goal line

scoring.winning: Team with most goals after 2 periods wins

expulsion_foul.description: Ejection from game for flagrant infractions

violations: Non-goalie in crease during live play
Goal cancelled if offensive player in crease when goal scored
Defensive player in crease is 1-yard foul
goalie_privileges: Only goalie can move freely in crease
Can throw ball out to clear

clearing.definition: Moving ball from defensive end past midfield
clearing.emphasis: Less emphasis than standard due to shot clock pressure

key_differences_from_standard: Smaller field (70x36 yards) emphasizes 1v1 skills and spacing
Free substitution rules enable higher tempo
Higher shooting frequency and possessions per game
Faster game flow and continuous substitution
style_of_play: Emphasis on individual skill (1v1 shooting, dodging)
All players must be capable scorers
More balanced attacking/defending from all players

strategic_considerations.spacing: Tight field requires careful spacing and movement
strategic_considerations.transition: Speed of transition is competitive advantage
strategic_considerations.conditioning: Athletes fatigue quickly; frequent substitution essential
strategic_considerations.positioning: One defensive mistake results in goal-scoring opportunity
strategic_considerations.shooting: Lower defensive pressure allows more outside shots

overtime.format: Sudden death, first team to score wins

coaching_notes.pace_management: Shot clock forces aggressive play; coaches must manage clock
coaching_notes.player_development: Excellent format for developing young players' decision-making
coaching_notes.conditioning: Requires excellent conditioning; frequent substitution recommended
coaching_notes.skill_emphasis: Develops shooting, 1v1 dodging, field awareness at accelerated rate
